title: Enumerate substrate-ID citations inside code comments (issue-012 tooling)
description: Write scripts/scan-comment-citations.ts: a standalone, informational (non-gate) script that walks every packages/*/src TypeScript file, uses the TS compiler API scanner (ts.createScanner with skipTrivia=false) to visit every comment token (JSDoc /** */, block /* */, line //) exactly once, and matches a citation regex against each comment's text to enumerate every substrate-tracker-ID-shaped token (FGAP-\d{3}, DEC-\d{4}, FEAT-\d{3}, TASK-\d{3}, VER-\d{3}, PHASE-[A-Z0-9-]+, MILE-\d{3,}, REVIEW-\d+, RAT-\d+, CTX-\d+, WO-\d+, STORY-\d+, PLAN-\d+, REQ-\d+, R-\d{4}, ISSUE-\d+/issue-\d+, JI-\d{3}) found in comments. Outputs JSON to stdout: scannedPackages, totalInstances, totalFiles, byId, byFile, and a full instances[] array (file, line, id, commentKind, snippet). This is the enumeration step needed before issue-012 (frozen-era ID citations in pi-agent-dispatch resolving to unrelated live substrate items) and the general ID-rot problem class can be resolved -- it establishes exactly how many such citations exist and where. Corrects two confirmed gaps in the existing citation-rot-scanner.ts pattern (which this script cannot import -- CITATION_RE is unexported and pi-context's package.json exports map has no subpath for that file): PHASE ids are alphanumeric slugs live today (PHASE-PORT-OPS etc.), not the numeric-only PHASE-\d{3,} that scanner uses; MILE-\d{3,} has no alternative at all in that scanner despite 9 live MILE-00N items. Scope discipline: enumeration and counting only -- this script does NOT resolve each cited ID against the live substrate to judge staleness; that is a distinct follow-on capability, out of scope here. Investigation basis: analysis produced during this session's issue-012 discussion (citation-rot-scanner.ts read in full; phase.schema.json / milestone.schema.json id patterns confirmed against live .context data).
acceptance_criteria: npx tsx scripts/scan-comment-citations.ts runs clean over the full monorepo (no crash) and produces valid JSON.

Runtime demo: the output's instances[] array contains FEAT-006, TASK-088, TASK-091, DEC-0044, DEC-0047, DEC-0018, DEC-0014 entries at the file/line locations issue-012 already cites in packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/work-order-loop.ts and run-work-order-loop-tool.ts -- this is the load-bearing correctness check, not just 'it ran'.

scripts/scan-comment-citations.test.ts exists with unit cells against synthetic fixtures (JSDoc with 2 citations, line comment with 1, zero-citation file) asserting exact instances/byId/totalInstances shape, plus a live-repo integration cell pinning the issue-012 FEAT-006/TASK-088/TASK-091 finding as a regression.
npm run build + npm run check + npm test exit 0 (the new test file runs via the existing scripts/*.test.ts glob in the test:migration script -- no new wiring required).

A fresh adversarial probe independently confirms the corrected PHASE-[A-Z0-9-]+ and MILE-\d{3,} patterns actually match real live IDs of those families (or confirms zero such citations exist in comments today, not that the pattern silently can't match them) -- scoped strictly to enumeration accuracy, not broader.
The script is not wired into npm run check, husky, or CI -- it is informational tooling only; making it a gate is an explicit future decision, not assumed here.
